name: Marketing YouTube Tutorial
description: Plan a YouTube tutorial: script + storyboard + thumbnail concept + TTS narration audio. Lawrence does the actual screen recording in the morning hour; this pipeline does everything else.

config.userPrompt:
You are planning a tutorial video for the mChatAI YouTube channel. Target playlist: 5-Minute Builds. Audience: indie creators and prosumer Mac users who want to build games, tools, podcasts, and automations using AI. Voice: warm, direct, helpful, no hype. ASCII only.

Total video length: 4 to 7 minutes. Format: 60s setup, 3 to 4 minutes build, 60s polish + call to action.

Given the topic below, produce ONE markdown document with these EXACT sections (use these H2 headings):

## Title
One line. Max 60 chars. Outcome-led. No clickbait. No question marks. Example: 'Build a Wordle clone on your Mac in 90 seconds'.

## Hook (first 15 seconds, on-camera)
What the viewer sees and hears in the first 15 seconds. Two sentences max. Establish the outcome (what they will have built by the end) and the time investment.

## Storyboard
Numbered scene list. For each scene give: timestamp range, what is on screen (specific UI surface in mChatAI), what the host says (one sentence), and any callouts to overlay in post. 8 to 14 scenes. Use this exact row format:

  Scene 1 (0:00 - 0:15) | ON SCREEN: <thing> | SAYS: <one sentence> | OVERLAY: <text or none>

## Full Narration Script
The complete spoken transcript, paragraph form. This is what the host says, no markdown, no headings, no bracketed directions. Open with the hook. End with a clear call to action: a specific URL or recipe to try. 600 to 1000 words.

## Thumbnail Concept
Two to three sentence visual description for a 1280x720 thumbnail. Specify: primary subject, color palette (3 colors), text overlay (max 5 words, no punctuation), composition. Optimised for the YouTube grid (mobile + dark mode preview).

## Description (YouTube field)
3 short paragraphs. First paragraph: what the viewer will build, who it is for. Second: the prompt or recipe used inside mChatAI. Third: where to go next. Max 1200 chars total.

## Tags (YouTube field)
Comma-separated list. 10 to 15 lowercase tags. Mix product (mchatai, macos, ai-tools) with topic (e.g. wordle, web-game, javascript).

## Chapters (YouTube field)
Timestamps + chapter titles, one per line, in this exact format (YouTube auto-detects):

  0:00 Hook
  0:15 Setup
  1:00 Build the thing
  ...

General rules:
- ASCII only. No smart quotes, no em-dashes (use ' - ' surrounded by spaces). No curly apostrophes.
- Do NOT invent product features or stats.
- Names: mChatAI (capital A, capital I; no space). Product surfaces: AIWizard, Pipelines, Recipes, Skills, Agents, Hub, mChatAIShell.
- Skip phrases: 'in today's fast-paced world', 'unlock', 'game-changer', 'thrilled to share'.

TOPIC:
{{INPUT}}

Extract ONLY the Full Narration Script section from the document below, and convert it into a spoken transcript optimised for text-to-speech. ASCII only.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the spoken text. No markdown, no headings, no bullet points, no bracketed directions, no scene numbers.
- Use natural conversational transitions ('next', 'here is the move', 'one thing to notice', 'and that is it').
- Spell out numbers under ten. Spell out abbreviations on first mention.
- For URLs and product names, say 'm chat a i dot com slash recipes', not the raw URL. Say 'a i wizard', not 'AIWizard'.
- Use contractions naturally (it's, you're, that's, here's).
- Keep the length close to the original (600 to 1000 spoken words).

DOCUMENT:
{{INPUT}}
